summary: City Safety Simulation feature fully tested. All 12 requirements passed. Frontend: Simulation section renders correctly between Live Threat Intelligence and City Safety Operations. Shows 'AI Safety Network Simulation' label, 'Run City Safety Simulation' button in idle state, 5-step animation with proper badges (MONITORING, ANOMALY, ALERT, RESPONDING, RESOLVED), step counter, message panel, progress bar, and 'Replay Simulation' button on completion. 12 labeled nodes visible (School, University, Transit Hub, etc.). Backend: Chatbot responds to 'city simulation/simulate/grid' keywords with link to homepage. All existing homepage sections still render correctly.

Frontend_CitySafetySimulation.Step_5_RESOLVED: PASS - Badge shows 'RESOLVED', Step 5/5, correct message

Frontend_CitySafetySimulation.12_node_labels: PASS - All 12 labels found: School, University, Transit Hub, Market District, Academy, Tech Park, Western Corridor, City Center, High School, Hospital Zone, Rail Station, Residential
Frontend_CitySafetySimulation.Section_placement: PASS - Between Live Threat Intelligence (y=1354) and City Safety Operations (y=2633)

critical_code_review_comments: CitySafetySimulation.jsx is well-structured with proper constants for NODES, EDGES, STEPS, colors
All interactive elements have proper data-testid attributes for testing
Node colors change appropriately: green(safe) → orange/red(warning/alert) → cyan(responding) → green(resolved)
Chatbot keyword detection in chatbot.py handles: city + (simulation|simulate|grid)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all simulation features working, all homepage sections rendering)

context_for_next_testing_agent: City Safety Simulation feature complete. Component at /app/frontend/src/components/CitySafetySimulation.jsx renders a 5-step animated city grid with 12 nodes. Simulation auto-plays ~22 seconds. Chatbot keywords: 'city' + ('simulation'|'simulate'|'grid'). Section data-testid='city-simulation-section', container data-testid='city-safety-simulation', start button data-testid='sim-start-btn', reset button data-testid='sim-reset-btn'.
